sglang.title: Use rocminfo instead of rocm-smi for more OS/WSL support
sglang.author: HaiShaw
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Address #2213
Modifications
Use rocminfo instead of rocm-smi for broader OS support.

Checklist

[+] Format your code according to the Contributor Guide.
[+] Add unit tests as outlined in the Contributor Guide.
[+] Update documentation as needed, including docstrings or example tutorials.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Update `mistral_common` version for tests and docs
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #10367 updated mistral_common only in requirements-common.txt. To maintain consistency, this PR updates it for the other files as well.

vllm.title: [misc] use out argument for flash attention
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
replace #9740 since that is very old.
To make mypy happy, all attention forward signature needs to match. so i add output: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None, for all attention.

vllm.title: [Misc][LoRA] Move the implementation of lora bias to punica.py
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Moving the  lora bias calculation code from layers.py and fully_sharded_layers.py to punica.py.
The purpose is to consolidate all lora-related calculations in  punica.py, making it easier to support lora on different hardware platforms in the future.
cc @sanjucsudhakaran

vllm.title: [Misc][XPU] Avoid torch compile for XPU platform
vllm.author: yma11
vllm.bodyExcerpt: After torch.jit.script --> torch.compile in PR, triton is eventually called during tensor parallel on XPU platform. We need disable torch.compile() for xpu.

vllm.title: Fix openvino on GPU
vllm.author: janimo
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Handle recent signature change of dummy_data_for_profiling in the openvino worker.

vllm.title: [Model] Add TP and BNB quantization support to LlavaMultiModalProjector
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
I just realized that we still can't load unsloth/llava-1.5-7b-hf-bnb-4bit mentioned in #10813, because it has llava multimodal projector quantized as well. 😅

Add TP and quantization support to llava multimodal projector
Enable uninitialized weights tracking for BNB model loader

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Prevent benchmark_throughput.py from using duplicated random prompts
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently this benchmark script is misleading performance for V1 (as in the performance is too good) since prefix caching is enabled by default and all of the prompts in the default case are identical.
Before this PR:
VLLM_USE_V1=1 python benchmarks/benchmark_throughput.py --model meta-llama/Llama-3

vllm.title: [Model] support bitsandbytes quantization with minicpm model
vllm.author: zixuanzhang226

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix QKVParallelLinearWithShardedLora bias bug
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix  QKVParallelLinearWithShardedLora bias bug ,which was  introduced by #10829
Remove  lora soft_failtag

vllm.title: [torch.compile] remove compilation_context and simplify code
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: remove the confusing compilation context, figure out cudagraph batchsizes during initialization of the config.

vllm.title: [Doc] Add github links for source code references
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, the use of sphinx.ext.viewcode generated new pages that
included the raw source code. This change adds the
sphinx.ext.linkcode extension, which allows defining a custom method
for generating source code URLs given a code reference. This now
includes links to the appropriate file and line

vllm.title: [Misc] Remove deprecated names
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR removes the deprecated identifiers vllm.inputs.PromptInput, vllm.inputs.LLMInputs, vllm.inputs.EncoderDecoderLLMInputs, and vllm.multimodal.MultiModalInputs, all of which have been renamed.
It also introduces the deprecated decorator to other deprecated functions so that type checkers can em

vllm.title: [Core][Performance] Add XGrammar support for guided decoding and set it as default
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add initial support for XGrammar for V0 and makes it the default for grammar and json usage. Written in collaboration with @mgoin
I'm using the benchmark scripts from #10557
Results for using XGrammar as backend:
Throughput: 0.94 requests/s, 1022.46 total tokens/s, 480.27 output tokens/s Correct rat
